_comment: Illustrative LLM output. Actual persona content will vary.

personas.name: The Activation-Risk Casual
personas.primary_goal: Track my team's tasks without spending time on setup or maintenance
secondary_goals: Get a quick status overview without opening the app
Share progress with a founder or client without manual work
key_frustrations: Onboarding takes too long — unclear where to start
Too many features I never use, making the UI feel cluttered
Mobile app doesn't let me do the basics on the go
behavioural_patterns: Creates tasks but rarely uses due dates or assignees
Checks status once per week, not daily
Has not discovered recurring tasks or dependencies
personas.success_metric: Team is aligned on what's happening this week without a meeting
personas.retention_signal: Whether they discover and use recurring tasks within the first 30 days — accounts that do have 3x retention
product_implications: Onboarding must surface recurring tasks early for this persona
Mobile UX must support the basic task+status flow without friction
Empty states and tooltips matter — this persona does not read docs
research_evidence: NPS survey: casual users only create tasks and update status, high churn
Feature analytics: recurring tasks not used — but usage predicts retention

personas.name: The Collaborative Team Lead
personas.primary_goal: Keep the team aligned across multiple concurrent workstreams without spending all day in meetings
secondary_goals: Get non-technical stakeholders to update their own tasks
Reduce notification noise without losing visibility
key_frustrations: Non-PM team members resist adopting the tool
No easy way to share a project view with someone who doesn't need to edit
behavioural_patterns: Daily active user across web and mobile
Uses task dependencies and recurring tasks
personas.success_metric: Everyone on the team updates their own tasks, and I can get a status overview in under 2 minutes
personas.retention_signal: Whether more than 60% of invited team members actively contribute tasks — accounts where only the PM uses it churn in 12 months
product_implications: Notification controls per project are critical for this persona
Read-only sharing links would remove a key adoption blocker
Team onboarding (not just individual) needs its own flow
research_evidence: Customer interviews: mid-market PMs care about team adoption and collaboration
NPS survey: team of 10+ people, too many notifications

personas.name: The Enterprise Power User
personas.primary_goal: Manage 5+ concurrent enterprise projects with full visibility into dependencies, capacity, and blockers
secondary_goals: Produce status reports for executives without manual effort
Integrate project data into the tools the rest of the company already uses
key_frustrations: No RBAC — can't control what different roles can see or edit
CSV export is incomplete — still requires manual report assembly
No Microsoft Teams integration — forces tool-switching
behavioural_patterns: Uses task dependencies for every project
Exports data weekly for external reporting
Manages user permissions manually — workaround for missing RBAC
personas.success_metric: Zero project surprise escalations — everything tracked, assigned, and visible before it becomes a problem
personas.retention_signal: Whether enterprise security features (RBAC, audit log) are in place — this persona churns at contract renewal without them
product_implications: RBAC is a blocker — this persona cannot fully commit without it
Microsoft Teams integration is a deal requirement, not a nice-to-have
Reporting and export quality directly affects renewal conversations
research_evidence: Customer interviews: enterprise PMs care about integrations and reporting
Feature analytics: dependencies used by 12% of accounts, all in Segment C

result.persona_matrix: The three personas represent a natural customer progression: Casual Activators churn early if not converted to power behaviour; Collaborative Team Leads are the growth engine and represent the largest revenue opportunity; Enterprise Power Users are the highest-LTV segment but require enterprise features (RBAC, integrations) to stay. Retention strategy should focus on activating Casuals into Team Leads, and converting Team Leads into Power Users.
